QF.id: Analysis of Equity and Debt Instruments_1

question.text: Priya was allotted 500 ESOPs by her listed company on 15-Mar-2024 at an exercise price of Rs.100 per share. The FMV on the exercise date was Rs.450 per share. She sold all shares on 20-Apr-2025 at Rs.600 per share. What is the total capital gain arising on the sale?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For ESOPs, the cost of acquisition for capital gains purposes is the FMV on the exercise/allotment date, i.e., Rs.450 per share. Capital gain = Sale price - FMV on exercise date = Rs.600 - Rs.450 = Rs.150 per share. Total = 500 × Rs.150 = Rs.75,000. Holding period is from allotment date (15-Mar-2024) to sale date (20-Apr-2025) = more than 12 months for listed shares, so it is LTCG. **The Trap:** Rs.2,50,000 uses exercise price (Rs.100) as cost instead of FMV (Rs.450). The perquisite component (Rs.450 - Rs.100 = Rs.350 per share) is separately taxed as salary income at the exercise stage. Using Rs.100 as cost would double-count this amount. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (MF) and Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) also test basic capital gains concepts but not ESOP-specific two-stage taxation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind clients that ESOP taxation is a two-stage process — perquisite tax at exercise and capital gains at sale. The FMV on exercise date becomes the cost base for capital gains.
